content:
{
  "title": "Self-Discrepancy Prompting: Contextual Consistency Probes for Robust LLM Defense",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) remain vulnerable to adversarial prompts and jailbreak attempts that bypass safety guardrails. Current prompting-based defenses struggle to detect subtle adversarial intent embedded in complex, context-aware attacks.",
  "motivation": "With the proliferation of LLM-driven applications, ensuring robustness against sophisticated jailbreaks is critical for trustworthy AI. Existing methods either rely on explicit instruction filtering or fixed shield prompts, which adversaries can circumvent. Dynamic, context-sensitive strategies are needed to detect and mitigate nuanced attacks in real time.",
  "method": "We propose Self-Discrepancy Prompting (SDP), a prompting method where the LLM is presented with minimally modified, context-preserving variations of the user prompt (e.g., tense, formality, or semantic paraphrasing) as ‘consistency probes’ alongside the original. By comparing the model's outputs for these variants, deviations in safety-critical behavior are used to flag adversarial intent. Unlike shield prompting or static filters, SDP leverages the LLM’s own context understanding to dynamically surface inconsistencies indicative of jailbreak attempts, even for unseen or obfuscated attacks.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate SDP on jailbreak and adversarial prompt datasets (e.g., JailbreakBench, custom adversarial sets) across open and closed LLMs. Baselines include AdaShield, static filtering, and prior prompting defenses. Metrics will include attack success rate reduction, false positive rate, and detection latency."
}
